task_completion.description: Log into OpenClinica as the root user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenClinica dashboard or interface is visible, indicating a successful login (e.g., 'Welcome, root' or similar user indicator).
A dataset creation screen or dataset list showing the name 'SAS_Efficacy_Data' entered in the dataset name field or listed as a created dataset.

task_completion.description: Include 'Vital Signs' and 'Lab Results' CRFs in the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dataset item selection screen shows 'Vital Signs' and 'Lab Results' checked or listed in the selected items panel for the dataset.

task_completion.description: Ensure 'Demographics' and 'Adverse Events' CRFs are excluded from the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dataset item selection screen shows 'Demographics' and 'Adverse Events' unchecked or absent from the selected items panel.

task_completion.description: Export the dataset in 'SAS data and syntax' format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The export dataset page shows 'SAS data and syntax' selected, or a download button/link for the SAS format is clicked or generated.

task_completion.description: Extract the contents of the exported archive to ~/Documents/SAS_Analysis/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/SAS_Analysis' containing the extracted files (e.g., .sas syntax files and data files).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenClinica web interface to create and export the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenClinica UI, clicking through dataset creation and export steps, rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent downloaded the export archive through the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: A browser download prompt, download history, or file manager showing the downloaded .zip file in the Downloads folder before extraction.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct PostgreSQL queries to create the dataset or mock the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing 'psql' commands injecting dataset records or manually creating the SAS files using echo/cat commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset must be exactly named 'SAS_Efficacy_Data'. It must include the CRFs 'Vital Signs' and 'Lab Results', and explicitly exclude 'Demographics' and 'Adverse Events'. The final extracted files must be located in the directory '/home/ga/Documents/SAS_Analysis'.
